mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with expanding retail sales indicates synchronized supply-demand recovery, which compresses input cost volatility and boosts corporate margin expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns do not exceed the baseline median by at least 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where industrial output peaks while capacity utilization remains elevated, supply-side bottlenecks trigger inflationary pressure that forces monetary tightening, which subsequently compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in over 60% of late-cycle peak instances, or CPIAUCSL fails to accelerate when INDPRO plateaus.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth signals expanding aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate revenue trajectories and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings momentum prices into valuations.
failure_condition: The signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or forward returns show no statistical difference from a random walk benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, borrowing costs suppress corporate investment and consumer credit demand, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal exhibits a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates broad-based economic acceleration, which strengthens consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants price in robust earnings growth.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns do not exceed the baseline median by at least 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: During a disinflationary growth environment where CPIAUCSL decelerates while GDPC1 expands, real purchasing power rises and corporate margins improve, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative in over 50% of confirmed disinflationary growth instances, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3.5% annually.
